mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand as discount rates compress and real yields turn deeply negative, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% as of 2021-01-15 indicates emergency accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window during zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 500k signals robust labor market recovery and consumer spending capacity. This drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise and recession risk declines.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,071k as of 2021-01-15 shows post-pandemic recovery trajectory
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when PAYEMS growth exceeds 500k month-over-month, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth combined with housing starts (HOUST) above 1,100k units signals synchronized consumer and residential demand strength. This joint signal predicts positive 20-day equity returns as forward earnings guidance improves across consumer-sensitive sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,174k and RSAFS at 446,477M as of 2021-01-15
failure_condition: When both HOUST > 1100 and RSAFS growth > 0.5%, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity markets reprrice earnings downward in response to manufacturing contraction.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines month-over-month by more than 0.3%, or Rank IC is positive and exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth above 2.5% combined with FEDFUNDS still below 1.0% creates negative real rates that erode bond returns and redirect capital to equities. This mechanism predicts positive 60-day SPX returns as portfolio reallocation accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.786 and FEDFUNDS at 0.13% as of 2021-01-15
regime_hint: negative real rates with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: When negative real rates persist (CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%), SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns as equity markets reprice downward earnings and increase risk premiums.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp over 3 months, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is positive and exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at annualized rate above 3.0% signals strong economic expansion. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns as earnings growth accelerates and recession probability declines.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,270.4B (2017 dollars) as of 2020-10-15 reflects post-pandemic recovery
failure_condition: When annualized GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60-day returns is below 0.12.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals synchronized supply and labor expansion. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns as production capacity utilization rises and unit labor costs stabilize, supporting profit margins.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% and PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 200k
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.477 and PAYEMS at 142,071k as of 2021-01-15
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow simultaneously (INDPRO > 0.5% MoM and PAYEMS > 200k MoM), SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
